zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Swynnerton於1341年出生在英國斯塔福爾斯恩頓時，他的父親Thomas de Swinnerton32歲，母親Maud de Holand31歲。他於1364年與Elizabeth Beke在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾斯恩頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1387年4月7日在法國布列塔尼布萊斯特享年46歲，埋葬在英國斯塔福爾斯恩頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert de Swynnerton于1341年出生在英国斯塔福爾斯恩頓时，他的父亲Thomas de Swinnerton32岁，母亲Maud de Holand31岁。他于1364年与Elizabeth Beke在英国英格兰斯塔福爾斯恩頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1387年4月7日在法国布列塔尼布萊斯特享年46岁，埋葬在英国斯塔福爾斯恩頓。

nameMeanings.name: Swynnerton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Swinnerton
meanings: English: habitational name from Swynnerton in Staffordshire. The etymology of the placename is uncertain. It may derive from Old English swille ‘sloppy mess, swill’ + ford ‘ford’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Alternatively, the first element may be swīn ‘swine, hog’, swān ‘herdsman, peasant’, swan ‘swan’, or sumor ‘summer’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Swynnerton was born in 1341, in Swynnerton, Staffordshire, England, his father, Thomas de Swinnerton, was 32 and his mother, Maud de Holand, was 31. He married Elizabeth Beke in 1364, in Swynnerton, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 7 April 1387, in Brest, Brittany, France, at the age of 46, and was buried in Swynnerton, Staffordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Swynnerton est né en 1341, à Swynnerton, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas de Swinnerton, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Maud de Holand, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Beke en 1364, à Swynnerton, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 7 avril 1387, à Brest, Bretagne, France, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterré à Swynnerton, Staffordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Swynnerton 1341 in Swynnerton, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas de Swinnerton 32 und seine Mutter Maud de Holand 31. Er hat 1364 in Swynnerton, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom Elizabeth Beke geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 7. April 1387 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Brest, Bretagne, Frankreich und wurde in Swynnerton, Staffordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Swynnerton è nato il nel 1341 nel Swynnerton, Staffordshire, England, il suo padre, Thomas de Swinnerton, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Maud de Holand, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Beke nel 1364, nel Swynnerton, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 7 aprile 1387, nel Brest, Bretagne, Francia, all'età di 46 anni, e fu sepolto a Swynnerton, Staffordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Swynnertonが1341年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアスウイネルトンで生まれた時，父Thomas de Swinnertonは32歳，母Maud de Holandは31歳であった。1364年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアスウイネルトンでElizabeth Bekeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1387年4月7日にフランスブレタグンブレストで46歳で死去し，イギリススタフォードシュアイアスウイネルトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Swynnerton(이)가 1341년 영국 스태퍼드실르 스윈르턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas de Swinnerton(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud de Holand(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1364년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 스윈르턴에서 Elizabeth Beke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1387년 4월 7일 프랑스 브레타그니 브레스트에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스태퍼드실르 스윈르턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Swynnerton nasceu em 1341, em Swynnerton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas de Swinnerton, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Holand, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Beke em 1364, em Swynnerton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 7 de abril de 1387, em Brest, Bretagne, França, com 46 anos, e foi sepultado em Swynnerton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Swynnerton родился в 1341 г. в местности свинниртон, стаффордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas de Swinnerton было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Maud de Holand, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Beke в 1364 г. в местности свинниртон, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 7 апреля 1387 г. в местности брест, бретагн, Франция в возрасте 46 лет и был похоронен в местности свинниртон, стаффордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Swynnerton nació en 1341, en Swynnerton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas de Swinnerton, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Maud de Holand, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Beke en 1364, en Swynnerton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 7 de abril de 1387, en Brest, Bretagne, Francia, a la edad de 46 años, y fue sepultado en Swynnerton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra.
